mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals monetary easing that lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the cut occurs after a prior tightening phase.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is lower than its value 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of FEDFUNDS-decline signals, or if the signal's walk-forward Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE reflects weakening labor demand and rising recession risk, which compresses expected corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes generate positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the 3-year rolling Rank IC is persistently above 0.03 with the wrong sign.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue momentum, which should support earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the sign of the 5-day forward return is negative more often than positive whenever RSAFS is above its 6-month moving average, or if the signal's rolling IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with a falling unemployment rate indicates genuine real activity expansion rather than inventory liquidation, which should support cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO/UNRATE signal does not outperform either proxy alone in walk-forward tests, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of triggered interaction cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals a housing-led demand slowdown that typically precedes weaker consumption and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by at least 10% versus its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of HOUST-drop episodes, or if the signal has no edge relative to an unconditional baseline over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is cooling while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy rates become less restrictive in expected terms and equity discounting improves, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS combination fails to beat the single-series FEDFUNDS signal in out-of-sample tests, or if positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of low-inflation/high-rate episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in PAYEMS after prior job losses signals labor-market stabilization and income support, which reduces recession probability and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases for 2 consecutive months after at least 3 months of decline
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 60% of qualifying PAYEMS recovery episodes, or if the signal is indistinguishable from noise in walk-forward Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, a simultaneous rise in FEDFUNDS and stagnant CPIAUCSL implies policy is becoming more restrictive in real terms, which should pressure valuation multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is not rising
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of qualifying tightening episodes, or if the interaction signal does not add predictive power beyond FEDFUNDS alone.
